english: ration of the months (of pregnancy) Dzl.; zla-dús ṭém-pa dań (zla dus them pa dang) at the expiration of those months Glr ; cf. also ṅo (ngo) 5. — 3. symb. num : 1. — Combinations and comp. zlá-bai dkyíl-ḳhor (zla ba'i dkyil 'khor), zla-dkyíl (zla dkyil), *da kyír-mo* W. disk of the moon; *da gań soń* W. the moon is full; *da gań-po (da gang po) or soń-te* W. zlá-ba rgyás-pa (zla ba rgyas pa) Pth., nya-rgyás zlá-ba (nya rgyas zla ba) Pth. full moon; nya (nya) day of full moon; zla(-ba) kám(-pa) (zla (ba) kam (pa)), zla-gám (zla gam), W. *da-p̣éd* half moon, i.e. the first and last quarter; semicircle, zlá-ba kám-pa ltá-bur bźág-go (zla ba kam pa lta bur bzhag go) they are placed round in a semicircle Do.; dbyibs zla-gám ltá-bur yod (dbyibs zla gam lta bur yod) it is semicircular in shape Glr.; zlá-bai ṅo (zla ba'i ngo) v. ṅo (ngo); zla-tśéb (zla tsheb) = zla-śól (zla shol); zla-nág (zla nag) new moon Sch. (?); zla-p̣ógs (zla phogs) monthly wages; zla-tsés (zla tshes) 1. = zlá-ba tsés-pa (zla ba tshes pa), tsés-gsum-zlá-ba (tshes gsum zla ba) Mil. the moon on the first two or three evenings of her being visible; crescent, zla-tsés ltá-bu (zla tshes lta bu) in the shape of a crescent, S.g.; it is also used as an image of speedy decay. — 2. date Schr.(?) — zla-mtsán (zla mtshan) the monthly courses; also the discharges of them, zla-mtsán dzag (zla mtshan dzag) the catamenial discharges flow Cs; zla-mtsán-can (zla mtshan can) Stg., zla-mtsán dań ldán-pa (zla mtshan dang ldan pa) S.g. having the monthly courses; zla(-ba)-śól (zla (ba) shol), -żól (-zhol), -ṭéb (-theb), zla-lhág (zla lhag), W. *da-ṭúl* intercalary month; the separate months of the year are usually counted from zlá-ba dáṅ-po (zla ba dang po) to bcu-γnyís-pa (bcu gnyis pa), yet there are also particular names for them, viz. acc. to Cs.: 1. brúg-zla ('brug zla), čuï zlá-ba (chu'i zla ba), rtá-pa zlá-ba (rta pa zla ba), माघ 2. sbrúl-zla (sbrul zla), ḳrá-zla (khra zla), dbó-zla (dbo zla), उत्तरफल्गुनी 3. rta(i) zla(-ba) (rta (i) zla (ba)), nág-zla (nag zla), चैत्र 4. lúg-zla (lug zla), sá-ga-zla-ba (sa ga zla ba), वैशाख 5. spré-zla (spre zla), snrón-zla (snron zla), ज्येष्ठ 6. byá-zla (bya zla), ču-stód-zla-ba (chu stod zla ba), पूर्वाषाढा 7. ḳyi-zla (khyi zla), gró-bżin-zla-ba (gro bzhin zla ba), उत्तराषाढा 8. p̣ág-zla (phag zla), ḳrúm-zla (khrum zla), भाद्रपदा 9. byí-zla (byi zla), ṭa-skár-zlá-ba (tha skar zla ba), आश्विनो 10. glań-zla (glang zla), smin-drúg-zla-ba (smin drug zla ba), कृत्तिका 11. stág-zla (stag zla), mgó-zla (mgo zla), मृगशिर 12. yós-zla (yos zla), rgyál-zla (rgyal zla), पौष II. vb., also zló-ba (zlo ba), zlós-pa (zlos pa), pf. bzlas (bzlas), bzlos (bzlos), fut. bzlo (bzlo), imp. zlos (zlos), 1. to say, tell, express. zlóam mi zló (zlo'am mi zlo) shall you tell it or not? Pth.; γzán-la zló-ba (gzan la zlo ba) Lex. to tell others; yid-ma-rańs-pa-nyíd p̣yír zlós-par byéd-pa (yid ma rangs pa nyid phyir zlos par byed pa) to express one's dissatisfaction Stg.(?). — 2. to murmur or mutter over, to recite softly or quite silently, prayers, spells etc., also żúb-bus zlá-ba (zhub bus zla ba) Zam.; yi-ge-drúg-pa lan-čig bzlás-pai bsód-nams (yi ge drug pa lan cig bzlas pa'i bsod nams) Glr. the merit of saying once the six-syllable prayer, and as such saying generally is done repeatedly, it is synon. with to repeat. — 3. to answer, reply Cs.; Mil. nif. — 4. undoubtedly a less correct spelling for ཟླ་བ་ zla-ba (zla ba) (for which reason the secondary forms with o are wanting), to pass, to get beyond, la zlá-ba (la zla ba) to cross a mountain-pass, nád-kyi la zlá-ba (nad kyi la zla ba) to be past hope of recovery Cs.; also trs., mya-ńán-las zlá-ba (mya ngan las zla ba) to deliver from pain, to help to eternal happiness.

1. = grogs (grogs), W. *yá-do*, companion, associate, zlá-bo byéd-pa (zla bo byed pa) to accompany, attend, assist, rkún-zla (rkun zla) a thief's accomplice Dzl.; °grán-zla (gran zla) rival, competitor (v. °grán-pa (gran pa) extr.); γnyén-zla (gnyen zla), v. γnyen (gnyen); bzá-zla (bza' zla) spouse, consort (male or female) Lex. - srid-zla (srid zla) Mil. partner for life; zla-γzán (zla gzan) a woman whose husband is dead ('who has eaten him'). - 2. friend, acquaintance B. and col. - 3. lover, bridegroom; spouse in C. To zla (zla) standing for zlá-bo (zla bo), may be referred zlas-dbyé (zlas dbye) Zam., expl. by द्वन्द्व, pair, couple, combination, viz. of a thing and its reverse, hence zlas-p̣yé-ba (zlas phye ba) reverse, contrary, e.g. yód-pai zlas-p̣yé-ba méd-pa (yod pa'i zlas phye ba med pa) Sch.

pf. bzlugs (bzlugs), fut. bzlug (bzlug), to give notice, send word, inform Sch., p̣rín-yig-gis bzlúgs-pa (phrin yig gis bzlugs pa) he informed him by a letter Stg., not frq.; in Lexx. explained by γzán-la snyád-pa (gzan la snyad pa), and gó-bar byed jùg-pa (go bar byed 'jug pa). —

1 adj., more frq. zlúm-po (zlum po), (= *kor-kór* C., *kyir-kyir* W.) round, circular, dbyibs in shape Glr.; roundish, rounded, obtuse, zlúm-por rtsig-pa (zlum por rtsig pa) to erect a round, cylindrical wall, e.g. for a monument; clubby, clumsy, e.g. of a short and thick tobacco-pipe; rkáṅ-pa zlúm-pa (rkang pa zlum pa) club-footed Stg.; globular, spherical, e.g. cavities in the human body S.g.; dku-zlúm (dku zlum) Zam.
